title: Speculation.
content:
Comes a train of little ladies
    From scholastic trammels free,
    Each a little bit afraid is,
    Wondering what the world can be!

    Is it but a world of trouble 
    Sadness set to song?
    Is its beauty but a bubble
    Bound to break ere long?

    Are its palaces and pleasures
    Fantasies that fade?
    And the glories of its treasures
    Shadow of a shade?

    Schoolgirls we, eighteen and under,
    From scholastic trammels free,
    And we wonder how we wonder! 
    What on earth the world can be!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Schwenck Gilbert delivers a powerful performance in "Speculation."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
